title: viola montana subsp. kuetzingii
intro: Viola montana subsp. kuetzingii, also known as the high mountain violet, is a subspecies of the Viola montana plant, which is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The high mountain violet has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and treating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
